JOHN MAHONEY APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. MICHAEL VARTAIN APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT STEPHENS INSTITUTE, INC. AFTER ARGUMENT, THE COURT ORDERED 3 DEPOSITIONS TO GO FORWARD. ALL DOCUMENTS NOT ASSERTED TO BE PRIVILEGE UNDER THE ATTORNEY CLIENT OR THE WORK PRODUCT DOCTRINE WILL BE PRODUCED AND A PRIVILEGE LOG WITH RESPECT TO THE PRIVILEGE DOCUMENT WILL BE PRODUCED IN ADVANCE OF THE 1ST DEPOSITION AND THE DOCUMENTS WILL BE PRODUCED AT THE DEPOSITION IN COMPLIANCE WITH THE NOTICE. THE COURT FURTHER ORDERED WITH RESPECT TO THE PROTECTIVE ORDER/QUASH MOTION, PLAINTIFF WILL SERVE WHATEVER LESS INTRUSIVE WRITTEN DISCOVERY AS SOON AS PRACTICAL AND HEARING WILL BE CONTINUED TO 11/9/10. ON THE NEXT HEARING DATE OF 11/9/10, WITH THE DISCOVERY PROPOUNDED AND ANSWERED, PARTIES WILL BE ABLE TO EVALUATE AND REPORT TO THE COURT WHETHER THE GOALS HAVE BEEN ACCOMPLISHED OR NOT. JUDGE: JAMES J. MCBRIDE; CLERK: R. VELUZ; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)

Notice And Motion For Order Compelling Compliance With Deposition Notice And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-18-2011 Text Ruling

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE - TRIAL COURTS
Off Calendar Feb-18-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on regularly as schuduled this day, having been continued from February 4, 2011, for hearing on the following motions:
(1) Defendant Stephens Institute, Inc.'s, dba Academy of Art (erroneously sued as Academy of Art Univerisity) motion for termination, issue, evidence and monetary sanctions against plaintiffs due to violations of Court Orders; (2) Plaintiffs' motion to compel compliance with deposition notice and for sanctions and (3) Case management conference.
Department 611. Judge: James J. McBride; Clerk: Kevin Dougherty; CSR Joe Vickstein, #4780.

In Pro Per plaintiff & cross-defendant Clay Kurtsinger appeared via telephone.
No appearance by plaintiff & cross-defendant Interfusion Technology.
No appearance by plaintiff & cross-defendant Monica Kurtsinger.
Martin J. Vartaine, Esq. appeared for defendant and cross-complainant Stephens Institute, Inc., dba Academy of Art (erroneously sued as Academy of Art University).

After hearing argument by plaintiff & defense counsel, and good cause appearing, the matters are submitted. Order on defendant's motions was submitted to the Court.

Notice Of Motion For Protective Order And To Appoint Referee And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-29-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, tentative ruling is adopted. Plaintiff Interfusion Technology, LLC's Motion for protective order and to appoint referee, and for sanctions granted in part, denied in part:
1. As to plaintiff's motion for protective order is granted as follows:
a. The deposition of Mr. Curtsinger shall resume on a date to be agreed upon by the parties, and to be held in Department 610 of this Court;
b. Deposition days may be reserved with the Discovery Clerk; depositions are conducted any business day between the 1:30 p.m. and 4:30 p.m.;
c. Subject to any future motion, inquiry concerning the financial condition of the parties shall be restricted pursuant to Civil Code 3295(c);
2. Motion to appoint referee, denied without prejudice;
3. Request for sanctions is ordered off calendar, pending the completion of the subject deposition. Prevailing party to submit form of order. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.
